global_rules.full_suite_rule: Required for leaves that touch live runtime behaviour, replay behaviour, or promotion-state surfaces.
global_rules.sequential_gate_rule: The next gate must not begin until the prior gate is complete, marked complete in this ledger and the execution log, and merged to main.

leaves.title: Define DMP v1 envelope and typed schema surface
leaves.scope: Create Desk Module Protocol v1 as the internal deterministic packet envelope for stage and module outputs, keeping the live stage payload models intact underneath.
implementation_tasks: Define a DMP envelope with protocol_version, packet identity, grammar role, behaviour class, schema identifiers, stack identity, coefficient identity, dependencies, trace references, trader summary, and payload.
Ensure DMP wraps existing typed cognition outputs instead of replacing them with loose dictionaries.
Write a short DMP v1 spec with explicit non-goals.
validation_tasks: Add schema tests for serialisation, required fields, and rejection of undeclared extras.
Prove stack_id and coefficient_set_id are first-class in the envelope.
leaves.pass_gate: A typed DMP envelope exists, is narrow, and serialises cleanly.
leaves.merge_gate: DMP is internal, repo-native, and does not rename or replace the existing cognition payload models.
acceptance_criteria: DMP v1 must carry stack and coefficient identity explicitly.
The payload surface must remain typed through the existing cognition schemas.
The spec must state clearly that MCP remains external and DMP remains internal.
stop_conditions: The design starts trying to solve every transport problem in one pass.
The envelope would force wholesale rewrites of existing typed payload models.
leaves.completion_note: Implemented in commit `b634dfa`, validated on branch, and merged to `main` via fast-forward at `02e5da6`.
completion_evidence: tests:make check -> ruff passed, mypy passed, pytest passed

leaves.title: Wrap the seven binding runtime stages in DMP without behaviour drift
leaves.scope: Add DMP wrappers for temporal, regime, options_flow, posture, eligibility, execution, and review outputs while preserving the existing runtime order and logic.
implementation_tasks: Emit DMP-wrapped stage packets in the existing runtime order.
Keep the current stage payloads and contract registry visible for downstream tests.
Do not change the decision logic of the current four playbooks during wrapper adoption.
validation_tasks: Prove the seven-stage order remains intact.
Prove the wrapped runtime still satisfies the existing runtime-contract registry tests.
leaves.pass_gate: All seven binding stages emit DMP packets and existing runtime behaviour still passes the contract tests.
leaves.merge_gate: All seven binding runtime stages emit DMP wrappers without behaviour drift under targeted tests and the full suite.
acceptance_criteria: Temporal, regime, options, posture, eligibility, execution, and review outputs must all be packetised in order.
The runtime contract registry must remain truthful.
Playbook output must not change merely because it is wrapped.
stop_conditions: DMP wrapping changes playbook decisions or execution styles.
The wrapper obscures the underlying typed payloads instead of clarifying them.
leaves.completion_note: Implemented in commit `11f2313`, validated on branch, and merged to `main` via fast-forward at `11f2313`.
completion_evidence: tests:make check -> ruff passed, mypy passed, pytest passed

leaves.title: Bind DMP trace references into review packets and replay artefacts
leaves.scope: Thread DMP packet references into the review and replay surfaces so comparison runs can cite exactly which packet chain produced a decision.
implementation_tasks: Attach packet references and packet lineage into review outputs.
Ensure replay and comparison artefacts can surface those references deterministically.
Keep comparison metrics stable while enriching traceability.
validation_tasks: Add tests that confirm review packets and replay results cite packet lineage.
Confirm comparison output remains serialisable and deterministic.
leaves.pass_gate: Important runtime decisions can be traced from review output back to stage packets and replay artefacts.
leaves.merge_gate: Review and replay artefacts carry DMP lineage without drift under targeted tests and the full suite.
acceptance_criteria: A review packet must be able to name the packet lineage that produced an allow, block, or reshape decision.
Replay artefacts must carry enough trace to compare configuration and packet ancestry honestly.
The comparison schema must remain deterministic.
stop_conditions: Trace lineage requires live vendor data to exist.
Replay metrics drift because trace attachment changed the decision logic.
leaves.completion_note: Implemented in commit `bfbe86e`, validated on branch, and merged to `main` via fast-forward at `bfbe86e`.
completion_evidence: tests:make check -> ruff passed, mypy passed, pytest passed

leaves.title: Define PlaybookSpec and ExecutionTemplateSpec without creating a second stack family
leaves.scope: Add typed playbook and execution-template schemas and a registry format that can represent the current live playbooks without touching StackDefinition or CoefficientSet.
implementation_tasks: Define PlaybookSpec and ExecutionTemplateSpec as first-class typed contracts.
Choose one checked-in registry format that matches existing repo config patterns.
Keep StackDefinition and CoefficientSet as the canonical stack/config surfaces.
validation_tasks: Add schema and round-trip registry tests.
Prove that playbook specs can express the current four live playbooks and their execution styles.
leaves.pass_gate: The repo has a typed, registry-backed playbook surface without stack-schema duplication.
leaves.merge_gate: Playbooks and execution templates become explicit artefacts while stack and coefficient ownership remain unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: The playbook registry must represent current live playbooks and execution templates cleanly.
A second overlapping stack schema must not be introduced.
The registry format must be checked in and testable offline.
stop_conditions: The design starts folding stack configuration into playbook specs.
The registry needs live system calls to load.
leaves.completion_note: Implemented in commit `2781950`, validated on branch, and merged to `main` via fast-forward at `2781950`.

leaves.title: Backfill the four live Gate-D playbooks into the registry
leaves.scope: Encode continuation_ladder, compression_breakout, pin_reversion, and negative_gamma_flush as checked-in playbook specs and execution-template specs.
implementation_tasks: Backfill the four live playbooks exactly as they behave today.
Encode their priority order, action biases, sizing fractions, hedge requirements, invalidation states, and exit-plan shapes.
Document any field that cannot yet be expressed and fix the schema rather than hiding it.
validation_tasks: Add parity fixtures for the four playbooks.
Ensure registry artefacts can express current execution styles without ambiguity.
leaves.pass_gate: All four live playbooks exist as checked-in registry artefacts and the registry surface can express their current behaviour.
leaves.merge_gate: The registry is faithful to the current code, not an aspirational rewrite.
acceptance_criteria: The four current playbooks must be encoded one-for-one in the registry.
Priority order must remain continuation_ladder, compression_breakout, pin_reversion, negative_gamma_flush throughout this plan.
This leaf must not add new playbooks.
stop_conditions: Backfill requires changing current runtime behaviour to fit the registry.
A live playbook cannot be represented without hiding an execution rule.
leaves.completion_note: Implemented in commit `4420b2e`, validated on branch, and merged to `main` via fast-forward at `4420b2e`.
completion_evidence: tests:.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_posture_risk_and_playbook.py tests/test_execution_review_runtime.py tests/test_playbook_registry.py -> 12 passed

leaves.title: Switch runtime playbook loading to the registry and prove no-drift parity
leaves.scope: Move the live playbook and execution-template lookup out of hard-coded service internals and into the checked-in registry, with parity tests proving no behaviour drift.
implementation_tasks: Load playbook and execution-template definitions from the checked-in registry.
Keep decision logic deterministic and current behaviour unchanged.
Add parity tests that compare registry-backed behaviour with the current fixture expectations.
validation_tasks: Run the existing Gate D and Gate F behaviour tests.
Add direct registry-parity tests for playbook ordering and execution style mapping.
leaves.pass_gate: The runtime loads the four live playbooks from the registry and existing behaviour remains unchanged under targeted tests and replay fixtures.
leaves.merge_gate: Registry migration is proven by parity, not assumed.
acceptance_criteria: Current playbook outputs must remain stable under the existing fixture scenarios.
Replay comparison results for the checked-in fixture pack must remain materially unchanged.
The registry-backed loader must become the only live playbook source of truth.
stop_conditions: Registry loading changes current outcomes and parity cannot explain why.
The migration requires hidden fallback logic that keeps the hard-coded paths alive.
leaves.completion_note: Implemented in commit `eb4a267` predecessor branch `d112766`, validated on branch, and merged to `main` via fast-forward closeout at `9a763de`.
completion_evidence: tests:.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_posture_risk_and_playbook.py tests/test_execution_review_runtime.py tests/test_replay_compare_runtime.py tests/test_runtime_parity_registry_playbooks.py -> 16 passed

leaves.title: Freeze contract-import tranche A manifest for the current live playbooks
leaves.scope: Select one bounded tranche of ready-for-contract-import items that materially deepen the current four-playbook runtime without opening a new ontology or live-vendor dependency swamp.
implementation_tasks: Freeze tranche A as thirteen named backlog items that directly support the current live playbooks and review surfaces.
Record each item's blockers, intended contract target, and whether the leaf outcome is concept-contract-only or implemented runtime.
Write explicit stop rules: no vendor feeds, no new ontology work, no broad playbook expansion, no silent promotion to approved runtime.
validation_tasks: Ensure every selected tranche item exists in the executable backlog report and remains ready_for_contract_import.
Ensure the manifest preserves grammar order and does not leapfrog into named playbook expansion.
leaves.pass_gate: One bounded tranche manifest exists with exact canonical IDs, blockers, contract targets, and stop rules.
leaves.merge_gate: The tranche is small enough to execute honestly and rich enough to deepen the current runtime.
acceptance_criteria: Tranche A must remain capped at the thirteen named backlog items listed in the canonical extension plan.
Every item must retain its backlog provenance and blocker list.
The manifest must separate concept-contract-only outcomes from implemented-runtime outcomes.
stop_conditions: The tranche expands beyond the frozen thirteen items.
An item would require live broker or live options-vendor access to continue honestly.
leaves.completion_note: Implemented in commit `eb4a267`, validated on branch, and merged to `main` via fast-forward at `eb4a267`.
completion_evidence: tests:make check -> ruff passed, mypy passed, pytest passed

leaves.title: Import tranche A upstream detectors as typed contracts with DMP envelopes

Prove imported outputs serialise through DMP and can be cited in review lineage.
leaves.pass_gate: The tranche-A upstream detectors exist as typed, DMP-emitting contract surfaces with explicit dependency fences.
leaves.merge_gate: Imported detectors deepen the runtime substrate without pretending that missing live-data dependencies are solved.
acceptance_criteria: The seven upstream tranche-A items must exist as typed contracts with packet outputs.
Imported detectors must not hide unsatisfied live-data dependencies.
Imported outputs must remain reviewable and replayable.
stop_conditions: An imported detector requires live vendor access to define its contract honestly.
The implementation starts bypassing the DMP envelope or the existing runtime order.
leaves.completion_note: Implemented in commit `c3600b6`, validated on branch, and merged to `main` via fast-forward at `c3600b6`.

Replay artefacts must be able to compare runs that include tranche-A contract surfaces.
Promotion state must remain honest and explicit.
